description_long: This is a large, traditional barn complex situated on a grassy plot, featuring a prominent central structure with a tall, multi-level monitor roof and attached lower wings. The main barn is constructed with red vertical siding and has a visible sign reading 'POOL' and additional text, suggesting it may have served a commercial or cooperative function. Several smaller outbuildings and extensions are connected by wooden walkways, creating a clustered arrangement typical of agricultural facilities. The roof is primarily gabled with a raised monitor section, and the siding pattern is vertical, giving the barn a classic rural appearance. There are a few small windows and doors distributed across the structures, with the main entrance likely at the front of the central building. The overall form and layout evoke the style of early 20th-century American monitor barns, designed for efficient storage and ventilation.
description: A large, red, multi-part barn complex with a tall monitor roof, vertical siding, and several attached outbuildings on a grassy base. The structure features a prominent central building with a sign, gabled and monitor roofs, and a clustered arrangement of smaller extensions.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main barn with a tall monitor roof, visible signage, and attached outbuildings on either side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the gabled roof of the main barn, the monitor section, and the clustered arrangement of smaller structures.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the depth of the barn complex, the vertical siding, and the spatial relationship between the main barn and outbuildings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the long side of the main barn, the monitor roof, and the grassy base with minimal outbuilding visibility.
attribute_roof_type: monitor roof with gabled lower sections
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door on the main barn, with smaller hinged doors on outbuildings
attribute_window_arrangement: few small windows, irregularly placed on the main barn and outbuildings
